question_html:
Re-write the correct form of the following sentences:

(a) He explained me the lesson.

(b) The scenery of Cox’s Bazar are beautiful.

(c) She is senior than me.

(d) I am used with cold weather.

(e) Neither of the two girls were absent.
solution_html:
(a) He explained the lesson to me.

(b) The scenery of Cox’s Bazar is beautiful.

(c) She is senior to me.

(d) I am used to cold weather.

(e) Neither of the two girls was absent.

Marks Distribution:

(0.5× 5) =2.5 marks for re-write the correct form of the following sentences

question_html:
What do you mean by the following words? Explain within one sentence.

Fatalist, Illiterate, Mentor, Misogynist, Vagabond
solution_html:
Fatalist – A person who believes events are predetermined and inevitable.

Illiterate – A person who cannot read or write.

Mentor – A wise and trusted adviser or guide.

Misogynist – A person who hates women.

Vagabond – A person who wanders without a home or job.

Marks Distribution:

(0.5× 5) =2.5 marks for Explain within one sentence.

question_html: Write down a short note on ‘The Role of Technology in Education’.
solution_html:
The Role of Technology in Education

Technology has brought a revolutionary change in the field of education. Modern tools like computers, the internet, multimedia, and smart classrooms have made learning more interactive and accessible. Students can now attend online classes, watch video lectures, and use digital libraries from anywhere in the world. Technology also helps teachers explain complex topics through visual presentations and practical demonstrations. In Bangladesh, initiatives like “Digital Bangladesh” have encouraged the use of e-learning platforms and mobile apps, making education more inclusive. However, over-dependence on technology may reduce creativity and human interaction. Still, when used wisely, technology enhances efficiency, saves time, and prepares students for the future. Thus, technology plays a vital role in improving the quality of education.

Marks Distribution:

0.5 mark for writing Title, “The Role of Technology in Education”

1.5 marks for writing, importance of The Role of Technology in Education.

1.5 marks for writing, tools of The Role of Technology in Education.

1.5 marks for writing, impacts of The Role of Technology in Education.

[Instructions:

* Students must write it within one single para. If it becomes more than one para, 50% marks have to be deducted from the obtained marks.

* Students must write it within 100 words limit. For exceeding the 100 words limit, 0.5 marks have to be deducted from the deserved marks.

* 0.5 mark will be deducted for one or two grammar or spelling mistakes, 01 mark for three or more grammar or spelling mistakes
